title: Sweet Springy Kenyan Lamb Skewers
description: A nostalgic Kenyan dish featuring lamb skewers marinated in a mixture of miso and Dutch stroop, perfect for a spring evening.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, Dutch stroop, olive oil, garlic, ginger, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, threading the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers if using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes before threading.
Grill the lamb skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Let the skewers rest for 5 minutes before serving, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Serve the skewers hot, accompanied by a side of roasted vegetables or a salad.
To enhance the flavors, let the skewers marinate for a longer period, up to 24 hours, before grilling.
For a spicy kick, add a diced jalapeno pepper to the marinade.
